honesty_note: Curated published survey figures, paraphrased for citation — not an independent primary dataset.

facts.claim: Organizations that put AI into production report an average ROI multiple of about 5.8x within roughly 14 months of go-live.
Industry adoption and realized ROI for AI vary by sector; retail and consumer-facing operations are active adopters but outcomes depend on use-case fit and operating model.
About 66% of enterprises report productivity gains from AI initiatives.
facts.source: Deloitte State of AI in the Enterprise

facts.claim: Revenue growth from AI remains aspirational for most firms: roughly 74% hope for revenue impact while only about 20% report achieving it.
facts.source: Deloitte State of AI in the Enterprise

facts.claim: Typical payback for agentic AI systems in finance is on the order of 8 months.
Typical payback for agentic AI systems in manufacturing is on the order of 12–14 months.
Customer-service chatbots commonly resolve about 68% of Tier-1 tickets when scoped to routine intents.

range_typical.note: Overall ticket deflection for mid-size support orgs is often lower than Tier-1-only rates; 20–35% overall is a common sanity band when Tier-1 is only a share of volume.

facts.source: Customer service chatbot resolution benchmarks (published industry figures)

facts.claim: True operating cost of an AI system is often 3–5x the initial build estimate once inference at scale, integration, and model-update cycles are included.

ROI = (Total Business Value − Total Cost) / Total Cost, where Total Cost includes build, inference at scale, integration, and model-update cycles. Integration and model-update costs scale non-linearly in year 2+ rather than staying flat.
